finding: assign_to_group(server_id, group.id) followed the wrapper's parameter NAMES, but the wrapper forwards positionally into assign_mcp_server_to_group(pool, group_id, server_id) — args swapped, so every group assignment failed with not_found(Server) and the 3 org servers were created but never made available to the Users group
fix: renamed the wrapper's params to the truth (group_id, server_id) — a pure rename, no behavior change, which also makes the 3 pre-existing callers correct-by-name — and fixed the call site

finding: mcp::init spawns run_startup_health_check, which probes every enabled non-built-in server and AUTO-DISABLES unreachable ones; the org servers get flipped enabled=false when their endpoints aren't up at ziee's boot, and ensure-mode never re-enables them
fix: shipped manifest declares the 3 servers mode: enforce (re-asserts enabled each deploy); documented in the module doc, the manifest and the README; tests set the debug-only ZIEE_DISABLE_MCP_HEALTH_CHECK seam so the probe can't race the assertions

finding: the compose file that IS the deploy shape (external managed Postgres, TeamCity) did not pass RCPA_MCP_URL/DSCC_MCP_URL/BIOGNOSIA_MCP_URL/ZIEE_ADMIN_PASSWORD/ZIEE_DEFAULT_USER_PASSWORD, so on the real deploy path the feature was inert
fix: added the five env passthroughs with empty defaults

finding: reconcile failed OPEN: an unreadable/unparseable file (e.g. Docker creating a DIRECTORY for a bind-mount of a missing host path) only logged and returned, so the container served with no admin — leaving the unauthenticated POST /api/app/setup/admin open to the first visitor — and with the permission trim silently not applied
fix: a bad FILE is now FATAL (reconcile returns Result; main.rs exits 1, lib.rs propagates); a bad ENTRY is still soft. Added a regular-file check (catches the bind-mount directory) and a 1 MiB size cap (catches a FIFO/char-device hang)

finding: GroupEntry.add was applied verbatim with no validation, so a typo'd or tampered manifest with add: ["*"] would grant the default group the grant-everything wildcard
fix: is_legal_add() refuses any add entry containing '*'; rejections are logged as errors and the shipped-manifest unit test asserts the file grants nothing

finding: MCP-server dedup is a non-atomic SELECT-then-INSERT and mcp_servers has no unique index on name, so two containers booting against one DB (rolling redeploy / --scale) both create rcpa/dscc/biognosia
fix: the whole reconcile now runs under a Postgres advisory lock (pg_advisory_lock), so concurrent boots serialize and the second sees the rows

finding: in ensure mode an existing server row returned BEFORE assign_groups, so a group assignment that failed on the first boot was never retried and a server could stay unusable by non-admins forever
fix: assign_groups now runs in both modes (assignment is additive + idempotent); documented that removing a group from the file does not revoke it

finding: the unit tests used unsafe std::env::set_var, which races concurrent getenv in other threads of the same test binary (that is why the API is unsafe) — the 'unique var names' comment does not prevent it, since the race is on the shared environ block
fix: resolve_with/resolve_secret_with take an injected lookup closure; the unit tests pass a HashMap and never touch process env

finding: the module doc + shipped manifest advertised `mode` on admin/users, but those structs are deny_unknown_fields with no mode field — an operator following the docs would make the WHOLE file fail to parse, skipping every entry
fix: AdminEntry/UserEntry now accept `mode` (documented as inert; an enforce value logs a warning explaining it is a no-op for accounts)

finding: the DELETE keyed only on (is_system, name), so it would also destroy an operator's OWN system MCP server merely named 'git' (plus its group assignments, OAuth config and approval history, all cascading)
fix: added the is_built_in = true guard — migration 25 stamps that flag on exactly the 4 seeded rows — and documented the cascade

finding: TEST-5/TEST-6 asserted enabled=true while the fire-and-forget boot health check was concurrently probing the unreachable fixture URLs and setting enabled=false — a coin-flip flake that was also masking the production auto-disable defect
fix: tests set ZIEE_DISABLE_MCP_HEALTH_CHECK=1 (the existing debug-only seam the e2e harness already uses)

finding: reboot() had no positive control: TEST-8/TEST-9 assert only the ABSENCE of change, so if the env plumbing to the second process silently broke, the second reconcile would be a no-op and both tests would stay green while proving nothing
fix: TEST-6 now deletes a declared server before the re-deploy and asserts it comes back — proving the second process really reconciled

finding: assert_eq!(assignments, 3, 'group assignments were duplicated') is unfalsifiable — user_group_mcp_servers has PRIMARY KEY (group_id, mcp_server_id), so duplicate rows cannot exist regardless of what the code does
fix: reworded + scoped to the Users group: it now pins that the re-deploy neither dropped an assignment nor added a spurious group

finding: the spec hard-coded its own trim SQL, so it would pass byte-for-byte with the entire desired_state module deleted, and its NOT LIKE 'hub::%' semantics diverged from the reconciler's (which also strips a bare 'hub')
fix: the spec now READS the remove: list out of the shipped config/desired-state.yaml and applies it with the reconciler's own prefix semantics, so a typo in the manifest fails the spec; the division of proof (integration = the reconciler writes it, e2e = the UI hides it) is documented in the spec header

finding: three false claims: 'ZIEE_DESIRED_STATE_FILE unset → skipped' (the image always sets it), 'removes projects::* which hides Projects' (the default group never had projects::*, so it is a no-op), and the enforce description ('the file wins over UI edits') which ignores that update_system_mcp_server is COALESCE-based so omitted fields keep their DB value
fix: all three corrected; added the health-check auto-disable note, the advisory-lock note, and the bad-file-is-fatal note

finding: hand-rolled sqlx::query! in a non-repository module, violating the convention that DB access goes through the repository layer
fix: added Repos.mcp.get_system_server_by_name() and used it; reconcile_mcp_server no longer needs a raw &PgPool

finding: comments in repository.rs + connection_health.rs still describe filesystem/browser/git as live built-ins, so a future reader could key new gating logic off names migration 157 removed
fix: updated the three comments

finding: with assistants::* removed, the chat composer's assistant picker is still rendered (it is not permission-gated) and dead-ends at 'No assistants available' for a trimmed user — Assistants is hidden from nav/settings/routes but not from chat
fix: NOT FIXED IN CODE — the human explicitly scoped this feature to a DB-permission change ('no delete anything, just set permission in the db, that's all'; DEC-8). Per the audit-vs-user-decision rule this is surfaced as an open question rather than silently reversed. Recorded as FB-3 in HUMAN_FEEDBACK.md

finding: resolve_secret enforces only the FORM ${VAR}, not that the var is a dedicated secret — password: ${HOME} passes the guard and yields a publicly-known password
fix: REJECTED: the desired-state file is trusted deploy config, at the same trust level as config.yaml (which holds jwt.secret in plaintext) and the compose file itself. An actor who can edit the manifest can already set any password directly via the env it names. A var-name denylist would add surface without removing a capability

finding: the gallery mock-API cassette still contains the deleted filesystem/browser/git rows
fix: REJECTED: the cassette is a RECORDED mock fixture for the backend-free component gallery — no gate reads the live DB from it and nothing asserts on those rows. Editing it would risk a Layer-B visual-baseline diff for zero behavioral gain; it rolls over on the next crawl

finding: reboot() binds 127.0.0.1:0, reads the port and drops the listener before spawning — a TOCTOU another parallel test could win
fix: REJECTED: this is exactly the pattern the SHARED harness itself uses to pick a port (tests/common/harness_inner.rs); adopting a different scheme here would diverge from the harness for no real gain, and a lost race fails loudly rather than silently
